summary: FQ-20 merges the HUD command-center/direct-manipulation pass with the painted-chrome pass per operator direction. A deterministic slicer (scripts/art/slice_hud_chrome.py) extracts thirteen production-quality painted assets from the operator's blueprint mockup (module frames, corner medallion, chip frame, riveted dock plate, slot frames, nav glyph buttons, and both orb rings with the glass punched for the runtime liquid), landing in a new ui_painted asset lane (manifest category, audit statuses, dedicated light verifier pass). hud.gd consumes all thirteen with full fallback chains, including an ornate NinePatch border on the mini-map. The five module toggles moved INTO the dock as chip buttons (the corner toolbar is retired) with two-way state sync, making the dock the player's single command center. Edit mode is now direct manipulation: drag any widget immediately (locks removed), continuous corner-grip resize 0.5x-2.0x, a full-screen overlay drawing gold outlines and grips, and layout schema v3. Suite 318/318; strict audit clean; pixel verifier 208; validator and capsule doctor pass; rendered tour reviewed with polish applied. Concurrent Codex work outside its wiki scope (opening cels, player-gear overlays) was detected mid-run, excluded from the commit, and flagged to the operator.
scope: FQ-20 only: slice_hud_chrome.py + ui_painted assets and lane, hud.gd chrome consumption + command center + direct manipulation, smoke evolution, docs. No inventory drag-drop (FQ-22 candidate), no modal-panel chrome, no changes to concurrent Codex lanes.
next_action: Operator reviews the painted chrome in motion; then scope FQ-21/22 (modal-panel chrome, Diablo-1 drag-drop inventory). Review the out-of-scope Codex writes (opening cels, player gear) in their own lane.
